delete_confirm_message: Are you sure you want to delete this goal?
no_goals_desc: Create a goal to start tracking your financial objectives.

card.quote_home: a place to come home to

dashboard.subtitle: Track and plan your financial goals
dashboard.empty_description: Create your first financial goal — whether it's retirement, a home, education, or anything else you're saving toward.

pending.calculating_help: Plan inputs are available now. Projection, coverage, and timeline widgets will load here when the calculation finishes.

value_mode.explained_aria: Today's value and nominal values explained
value_mode.todays_value_tip: Converts future dollars back into today's purchasing power, so amounts feel comparable to your current budget.
value_mode.nominal_tip: Shows the future dollar amount after inflation. This is the amount you would see in that future year.

dashboard.no_portfolio_data: No portfolio data found. Add accounts and holdings to see your retirement projection.

verdict.surplus_prefix: You're projected to retire at age {{age}} with

summary.capital_not_available: the required capital target is not available with the current assumptions. Review spending, inflation, returns, and life expectancy.

progress.capital_needed_tip_traditional: Capital needed at your retirement age after expenses, income, taxes, retirement returns, and fees. Shown in {{valueLabel}}.
progress.capital_needed_tip_fire: Capital needed at your desired retirement age after expenses, income, taxes, retirement returns, and fees. Shown in {{valueLabel}}.

chart.trajectory_tip_traditional: The retirement marker shows when withdrawals start. "What you'll need" is the minimum balance needed at each age to still fund planned retirement spending through life expectancy.
chart.trajectory_tip_fire: The FI marker shows the first sustainable age. "What you'll need" is the minimum balance needed at each age after crediting your remaining planned contributions. It only starts at today's portfolio if you are exactly on track. "What you'll have" is the projected portfolio path.
chart.required_tip_traditional: Minimum balance needed at each age to still retire at your target age and fund planned spending through life expectancy.
chart.required_tip_fire: Minimum balance needed at each age to still hit the desired retirement-age target after crediting the planned contributions you have left.

milestone.coast_hint: Stop contributing, still retires on time
milestone.coast_tip_nominal: Coast amount translated into nominal dollars at your desired retirement age.
milestone.coast_tip_real: Capital you need today that — with zero further contributions — grows to full FI by your retirement age.
milestone.lean_tip: Retire on a frugal budget — roughly 70% of your planned spending. Feasible earlier but leaves little margin.
milestone.fi_tip: Capital that funds your planned retirement spending through the full retirement horizon.
milestone.fat_tip: Retire with ~50% more spending than planned — room for travel, gifts, volatility, and lifestyle upgrades.

coverage.snapshot_suffix: spending and funding. Future items stay visible and are counted when active.
coverage.over_time_desc: How income and portfolio withdrawals cover planned spending through retirement.
coverage.over_time_tip: Values are shown in {{valueLabel}}. The stacked area shows how spending is funded; the dashed line is planned retirement spending.
coverage.no_income_active: No income active at age {{age}}; first income starts at {{nextAge}}.
coverage.draw_rate_tip: Gross portfolio withdrawal at this age, including estimated withdrawal taxes, divided by projected portfolio value. Informational only - it does not set your spending.
coverage.projection_unavailable: Coverage projection is unavailable for this plan.

disclaimer.title: One thing to keep in mind
disclaimer.body: This is not financial advice. Treat these numbers as a sketch, not a forecast. Everything here is a simulation built on the inputs you entered: returns, inflation, contributions, taxes, and how long you expect to live. Real markets don't move in straight lines, tax rules shift, and government programs get rewritten. Use this to stress-test ideas and spot gaps, then talk to a qualified professional before making decisions that are hard to undo.

warnings.high_inflation: High inflation assumption. Long-range spending needs become more sensitive at this rate.
warnings.high_fee: High fee assumption. Fee drag materially reduces long-term returns.
warnings.high_volatility: High volatility assumption. Outcome ranges can become very wide.
warnings.high_contribution_growth: High contribution growth assumption. This assumes sustained large savings increases.

plan.title: Plan inputs
plan.plan_type_help: Choose whether your target age is a traditional retirement date or the age you want to reach financial independence.
plan.mode_change_prefix: This changes the calculation model only.
plan.mode_change_traditional: Your desired retirement age becomes a fixed retirement start age.
plan.mode_change_fire: Your retirement age becomes the desired FI age; the planner will search for the first sustainable age.
plan.desired_retirement_age_hint: Age you want to become financially independent.
plan.horizon_hint: Age the plan should cover through.
plan.monthly_contribution_hint: How much you add each month until retirement.
plan.return_before_hint: Expected yearly return while you are saving.
plan.return_during_hint: Expected yearly return after withdrawals begin.

spending.title: Retirement Spending
spending.spending_type_help: Must-have spending is protected before flexible spending.
spending.inflation_override_help: Leave blank to use plan inflation.
spending.empty_hint: No spending items. Add a preset below, then adjust the amount and dates.

income.title: Retirement Income
income.public_pensions_note: Public pensions (e.g. CPP/OAS in Canada, Social Security in the US) aren't included automatically. Add them here if you want them in the projection.
income.income_type_help: Use income for pensions paid monthly. Use fund for balances that convert to an estimated payout (e.g. RRSP/RRIF in Canada, 401(k)/IRA in the US).
income.payout_estimate_note: Estimated payout uses {{pct}}%/yr of the projected fund balance unless you enter a monthly payout.
income.income_growth_help: Choose whether this income rises with plan inflation or stays fixed.
income.custom_annual_growth_help: Optional override for pensions or fixed-step benefits.

assumptions.title: Projection Assumptions
assumptions.annual_volatility_tip: How much yearly returns can vary around the expected return. Higher volatility widens the market-path outcome range.

taxes.title: Withdrawal Taxes
taxes.taxable_account_rate_tip: Effective tax rate applied when the planner withdraws from regular taxable or non-registered accounts. Higher rates increase gross withdrawals and can delay FI.
taxes.taxable_account_rate_edit_tip: Effective tax rate applied when withdrawing from taxable or non-registered accounts.
taxes.tax_deferred_account_rate_tip: Effective tax rate applied to RRSP, IRA, pension, or similar tax-deferred withdrawals. The planner grosses up withdrawals so spending is funded after taxes.
taxes.tax_deferred_account_rate_edit_tip: Effective tax rate applied to RRSP, IRA, pension, or similar withdrawals.
taxes.tax_free_account_rate_tip: Effective tax rate applied to TFSA, Roth, or similar tax-free withdrawals. This is usually 0%.
taxes.tax_free_account_rate_edit_tip: Effective tax rate applied to TFSA, Roth, or similar withdrawals. Usually 0%.
taxes.early_withdrawal_penalty_tip: Extra penalty applied to tax-deferred withdrawals before the cutoff age. Use 0% when the country or account wrapper has no early-withdrawal penalty.
taxes.early_withdrawal_penalty_edit_tip: Extra penalty applied to tax-deferred withdrawals before the cutoff age.
taxes.early_withdrawal_penalty_hint: Leave at 0% unless your jurisdiction applies an early-withdrawal penalty (e.g. US 10% before 59½). Canadian RRSPs don't use a separate penalty.
taxes.penalty_cutoff_age_tip_read: Age when the early-withdrawal penalty stops applying to tax-deferred withdrawals.
taxes.penalty_cutoff_age_edit_tip: Age when the early-withdrawal penalty stops applying. The tax rate above still applies after this age.
taxes.estimated_avg_withdrawal_tax_tip: Weighted average tax rate based on included account balances and each account bucket's withdrawal tax rate. This is a portfolio-level estimate; yearly withdrawals still follow the withdrawal order.
taxes.account_buckets_aria: More info about account tax buckets
taxes.account_buckets_tip: Value-weighted account shares used by the withdrawal engine. These come from the Account Shares section, not from the tax rate fields above.
taxes.taxable_bucket_tip: Portion of the retirement portfolio assigned to taxable or non-registered accounts.
taxes.tax_deferred_bucket_tip: Portion assigned to tax-deferred accounts such as RRSP, IRA, or pension-like accounts.
taxes.tax_free_bucket_tip: Portion assigned to tax-free accounts such as TFSA or Roth-style accounts.
taxes.zero_rates_note: Account bucket tags are applied, but 0% withdrawal rates mean no tax drag is modeled.

edit.title: Edit goal
edit.description: Update the goal name, notes, and status. Completed means the goal is done. Archived means you want to hide it from active goals without deleting it.
edit.description_retirement: Retirement assumptions, spending, taxes, and account shares stay in the planner.
edit.description_standard: Target amount, target date, and funding stay in the goal plan.
edit.goal_type_hint: Fixed after creation so the planner logic stays consistent.
edit.status_hint: Choose what should happen next: keep working on it, mark it done, or hide it from active planning.
edit.lifecycle_active_description: Shows in planning, progress, and active goal lists.
edit.lifecycle_achieved_description: Marks the goal complete and releases its assigned account shares.
edit.lifecycle_archived_description: Keeps the goal for reference, but removes it from active planning.

new.subtitle: Choose what you want to plan for
new.template_retirement_description: My life after work — and making it last
new.template_education_description: A fund for tuition, books, and courses
new.template_home_description: A down payment on a place of my own
new.retirement_exists_error: You already have an active retirement goal. Only one retirement goal is allowed.
new.retirement_exists_hint: You already have a retirement goal.
new.description_placeholder: Add a short note about this goal
new.planning_style_hint: Choose how you want to measure retirement readiness. You can change this later.
new.planner_traditional_hint: Plan around a specific retirement age
new.planner_fire_hint: Find when financial independence becomes possible
new.retirement_timeline_hint: Your birth month keeps your current age accurate over time.
new.independence_age_description: The age you would like work to become optional
new.retirement_age_description: The age you expect to stop working
new.funding_hint: You can choose funding accounts and monthly contributions after creation.

guide.title: Retirement guide
guide.subtitle: How Overview and What If work.

quick_start.title: Quick start — 5 steps
quick_start.step1_desc: In Plan inputs, choose the plan type, birth month, retirement age, life expectancy, and monthly contribution.
quick_start.step2_desc: In Retirement Spending, add the monthly spending lines you want the plan to fund. Use today's money; inflation is handled by the plan assumptions.
quick_start.step3_desc: In Retirement Income, add monthly income streams such as public pensions (e.g. CPP/OAS in Canada, Social Security in the US), workplace pensions, part-time work, or annuity income. Use Add pension fund only when the planner should estimate a payout from a fund balance at 3.5%/yr unless you enter a monthly payout yourself.
quick_start.step4_title: Check assumptions, taxes, and account shares
quick_start.step4_desc: Review projected returns, fees, volatility, inflation, withdrawal tax rates, tax buckets, and which accounts fund the goal.
quick_start.step5_title: Read Overview, then open What If
quick_start.step5_desc: Overview answers whether the base plan works and shows the trajectory, spending coverage, and plan inputs. What If tests the same plan across market paths, stress tests, decision maps, and advanced checks.

overview.title: Understanding Overview
overview.base_plan_desc: The hero summarizes the deterministic plan: whether spending is funded, whether the portfolio runs out before the planning horizon, and how the projected balance compares with the required capital at your target age.
overview.required_capital_desc: The estimated portfolio needed at your target age to fund the spending schedule you entered, after counting retirement income that starts later and estimated withdrawal taxes. It is a planning target, not a yearly spending rule.
overview.trajectory_desc: The solid line shows what you are projected to have. The dashed line shows what the plan is estimated to need over time. Use Today's value to read everything in today's money, or Nominal to include future inflation.
overview.coverage_desc: Your planned monthly retirement spending broken down by funding source. Income streams active at the selected retirement age appear as coloured segments; the remainder is what the portfolio must cover. Deferred streams show how the mix shifts at the age they start.
overview.input_cards_desc: The cards on the right are the editable inputs: Plan inputs, Retirement Spending, Retirement Income, Projection Assumptions, Withdrawal Taxes, and Account Shares.
overview.snapshot_desc: The table shows the projected end portfolio, yearly contribution, retirement income, planned spending, and portfolio withdrawal by age.

what_if.title: Understanding What If
what_if.market_paths_desc: Tests the same plan across many possible market paths using your return, volatility, and inflation assumptions. The shaded range shows bad-to-good outcomes; the line shows the middle path. Money lasts means the plan covers essential spending and still has money left through the planning horizon. In FIRE mode, the plan also needs to reach financial independence first.
what_if.base_case_desc: Shows the same deterministic base plan as Overview, then points to the largest stress result so you can see what matters most.
what_if.stress_tests_desc: Fixed checks show how the plan changes if returns are lower, inflation is higher, spending rises, retirement starts earlier, savings fall, or a market crash happens near retirement.
what_if.what_moves_desc: Decision maps compare savings, returns, retirement age, and spending. Green cells leave more money at the end. Red cells still leave a gap or run short.
what_if.crash_paths_desc: Compares a few crash-timing paths near retirement. A crash in the first retirement year can hurt more than the same crash later because withdrawals happen while the portfolio is down.

concepts.title: Key concepts
concepts.yearly_spending_desc: Your expenses drive the withdrawal. Each year the planner funds the spending plan you entered, subtracts retirement income, and estimates any tax drag on portfolio withdrawals.
concepts.target_calc_desc: The planner computes the present value of your full spending schedule from retirement through your planning horizon — accounting for each expense bucket's inflation rate, income streams that start at different ages, and tax drag on withdrawals. When your portfolio reaches this amount, the base plan has enough capital for the schedule you entered. This is more accurate than a simple multiple of expenses because it handles varying expenses, deferred pensions, and finite horizons.
concepts.inflation_desc: All projections grow expenses and inflation-linked income at this rate year over year. The default 2% matches the ECB target.
concepts.inflation_it_note: Use 2.5–3% for a more conservative Italian CPI assumption.
concepts.returns_desc: Return before retirement drives accumulation. Return during retirement drives the withdrawal phase and required capital. Annual investment fees are subtracted from both. Volatility is used only in market-path checks — higher values produce a wider fan of outcomes.
concepts.horizon_desc: How long the portfolio must last. The retirement target and What If checks run to this age. Set it to 90–95 to stress-test longevity. Early market crash paths and Money lasts are both measured at this horizon.

italian.title: Italian retirement setup (fondo pensione, TFR, INPS)
italian.portfolio_desc: This is your main portfolio — the value Wealthfolio tracks. Set your return assumptions, fee drag, and monthly contribution here. It is the primary accumulation engine of your retirement plan.
italian.fondo_desc: Use Add pension fund. Enter the current fund balance, monthly contribution, expected fund return, and payout start age. The planner estimates the future balance and, if you do not enter a monthly payout, estimates payout as 3.5%/yr of that projected balance from the start age.
italian.inps_desc: Use Add retirement income. Enter your estimated monthly net pension in today's money and set the payout start age. Use Indexed if you want the income to rise with the plan inflation assumption.
italian.tfr_title: TFR (Trattamento di Fine Rapporto — severance accrual)
italian.tfr_desc: If your TFR goes into a pension fund, include it in the monthly fund contribution. If you keep it separate, model it as another pension fund with its own balance, contribution, return, and payout start age. The same 3.5%/yr payout estimate applies unless you enter a monthly payout yourself.

detail.not_found_body: This goal could not be found.
detail.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{{title}}"? This action cannot be undone.
detail.no_plan_yet: This goal doesn't have a detailed plan yet.

funding.reserved_share_note: This share stays reserved while the goal is active. It is released when the goal is achieved or archived.
funding.accounts_exceed_share_one: {{count}} account exceeds available share. Use the highlighted max value before saving.
funding.accounts_exceed_share_other: {{count}} accounts exceed available share. Use the highlighted max value before saving.
funding.allocation_details_description: Active goal shares by account. Each account can be allocated up to 100%.

snapshot.title: Year-by-Year Snapshot
snapshot.pension_note: Pension fund balances are shown until payout starts. After that, the stream appears as retirement income.

skeleton.calculating_desc: Running stress tests, market-path simulations, and sensitivity maps against your plan. This usually takes a few seconds — results appear below as each section finishes.

save_up.summary_projected_prefix: this plan is projected to be
save_up.summary_set_target: set a target amount and date to see the projected gap.
save_up.configure_plan: Configure your plan to see projections.
save_up.hint_target_date: When you want this money available
save_up.hint_expected_annual_return: Set to 0% for pure cash goals

results.no_material_stress: None of these stress tests materially change your base plan.
results.money_left_drops_by: money left at the end drops by
results.money_lasts_definition_traditional: Money lasts means the plan covers essential spending and still has money left through age {{horizonAge}}.
results.money_lasts_definition_fire: Money lasts means the plan reaches financial independence, covers essential spending, and still has money left through age {{horizonAge}}.
results.money_lasts_prompt_traditional: Run 10k paths to see how often the plan stays funded through age {{horizonAge}}.
results.money_lasts_prompt_fire: Run 10k paths to see how often the plan reaches FI and stays funded through age {{horizonAge}}.

stress.eyebrow_with_count: What could break this plan? · {{count}} scenarios
stress.heading: What could break this plan?
stress.running: Running 6 stress scenarios against your plan…

montecarlo.heading: How often could the money last?
montecarlo.intro: We test the same plan across many possible market paths. The shaded range shows bad-to-good outcomes; the line shows the middle path.
montecarlo.running_detail: Testing many possible market paths. Results and the fan chart will appear when the run finishes.

moves.heading: What moves the plan?
moves.description: Shows how savings, returns, retirement age, and spending change the outcome. Green = more money left; red = shortfall or runs short.
moves.empty_title: See which changes would help most.
moves.empty_description: Compare saving more, earning a different return, spending less, or retiring at a different age.
moves.money_left_subtitle: Money left at the end, in today's dollars
moves.contribution_return_loading: Checking how saving and after-fee returns change the result.
moves.flat_column_hint: This age does not change much here because spending starts when the plan becomes financially independent.
moves.retirement_age_spending_loading: Checking how timing and retirement spending change the result.

advanced.heading: Early market crash paths
advanced.intro: Tests five crash-timing paths through retirement.
advanced.empty_description: Run the five paths to see which sequence would put the plan under pressure first.
advanced.unavailable_description: This check needs a positive projected portfolio at retirement start.
